name: Czechia
contextBlurb: Czechia applies the EU MiCA framework to stablecoins. MiCA classifies them as asset-referenced tokens (ARTs) and e-money tokens (EMTs). CNB is the national competent authority for all MiCA titles, licensing and supervising CASPs and overseeing ART/EMT matters. The Digital Finance Act (Act No. 31/2025 Coll.) took effect 15 Feb 2025, operationalising MiCA nationally. ART/EMT rules have applied EU-wide since 30 Jun 2024, with the rest of MiCA since 30 Dec 2024; Czechia opted for an 18-month transitional period for existing CASPs to 1 Jul 2026. MiCA sets reserve, disclosure, governance and redemption duties (EMTs: par-value in funds; ARTs: segregated reserve and market-value redemption). EBA takes over for significant ART/EMT issuers.

legislation.title: Apr 2026 ESMA confirmed that the MiCA transitional period ends mid-2026; crypto firms must hold a full MiCA/CASP licence by that deadline. As of April 2026, 199 CASPs are licensed across the EU; no Asset-Referenced Token (ART) issuer has yet been approved.
Deadline to apply for MiCA authorisation to benefit from the 18-month Czech grandfathering; transition runs until 1 Jul 2026.
ESMA issues supervisory findings to tighten MiCA authorisations, signalling closer scrutiny across the EU (relevant to Czech applicants).
Act No. 31/2025 Coll. takes effect; CNB is the national competent authority to license and supervise CASPs under MiCA.
Remaining MiCA provisions (incl. CASP rules and ESMA’s interim register) apply across the EU, covering platforms operating in Czechia.
MiCA Titles III/IV start applying EU-wide: issuers of EMTs/ARTs must meet reserve, governance and disclosure rules.

stablecoinMeta.summaryEn: Czechia applies the EU MiCA framework to stablecoins. MiCA classifies them as asset-referenced tokens (ARTs) and e-money tokens (EMTs). CNB is the national competent authority for all MiCA titles, licensing and supervising CASPs and overseeing ART/EMT matters. The Digital Finance Act (Act No. 31/2025 Coll.) took effect 15 Feb 2025, operationalising MiCA nationally. ART/EMT rules have applied EU-wide since 30 Jun 2024, with the rest of MiCA since 30 Dec 2024; Czechia opted for an 18-month transitional period for existing CASPs to 1 Jul 2026. MiCA sets reserve, disclosure, governance and redemption duties (EMTs: par-value in funds; ARTs: segregated reserve and market-value redemption). EBA takes over for significant ART/EMT issuers.
stablecoinMeta.classificationNote: Stablecoins in Czechia are governed by the EU MiCA regime and fall into two categories: e-money tokens (EMTs), which reference a single official currency and must be redeemable at par in funds by issuers that are credit or e-money institutions; and asset-referenced tokens (ARTs), which reference ano

regulators.name: Czech National Bank
regulators.role: Czechia’s MiCA national competent authority for all titles. Licenses and supervises CASPs and oversees ART/EMT matters; coordinates with EU bodies and maintains the national MiCA registers.

regulators.name: Financial Analytical Unit
